entities.name: TU Graz
entities.description: A technical university in Graz, Austria, partnering with Magna on the driving simulator project.

entities.name: Magna
entities.description: A mobility technology company that partnered with TU Graz and funded the simulator's acquisition and installation.

entities.name: Advanced Driving Simulation Center
entities.description: A new facility on Campus Inffeldgasse housing one of the world's most sophisticated driving simulators.

entities.name: Campus Inffeldgasse
entities.description: The campus location where the Advanced Driving Simulation Center is situated.

entities.name: Horst Bischof
entities.description: Rector of TU Graz who described the center as a result of the partnership with Magna.

entities.name: Arno Eichberger
entities.description: Head of the Institute of Automotive Engineering, responsible for the scientific operation of the testing facility.

entities.name: Institute of Automotive Engineering
entities.description: The TU Graz institute headed by Arno Eichberger overseeing the simulator's scientific operation.

entities.name: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems
entities.description: Vehicle systems (ADAS) that can be tested and optimised using the simulator.

entities.name: Austria
entities.description: The country positioned as an automotive and mobility centre enhanced by the new simulator.

entities.name: Europe
entities.description: The continent in which the research infrastructure is described as unique.
